Why Patients Seek a Second Opinion

A cancer diagnosis often raises important questions.

Cancer Clarity MD provides independent specialist review so patients and families can move forward with clarity and confidence.

Is the diagnosis correct?

Are there additional treatment options?

Are targeted therapies or clinical trials available?

Am I receiving the most up-to-date treatment?

Your Reviewing Specialist
Dr. Nikki Bajaj, MD

Independent specialist providing second opinions in benign and malignant hematology and oncology. Experienced in complex cancer diagnosis, treatment strategy, and emerging therapies. Dedicated to delivering clear, evidence-based guidance that empowers patients and families to make informed decisions about their care.

What sets us apart

Designed around patients - not institutions.

Dedicated Specialist Review

Your case is reviewed by a named, board-certified hematology-oncology physician - not a team, not a committee.

Structured Written Report

A clear, organized written second opinion designed to be shared directly with your care team.

Direct Video Consultation

Meet face-to-face with the reviewing physician to walk through findings and ask every question.

Emerging Therapies & Trials

Every review evaluates targeted therapies, emerging treatments, and potential clinical trial opportunities.

Global Access

No travel. No referrals. Consultations available to patients in the U.S. and internationally.

Technology That Supports Expert Care

Technology that supports expert care

Cancer Clarity MD operates through an AI-assisted concierge platform designed to simplify complex consultations. All clinical interpretation is performed by the reviewing physician.

AI-Assisted Preparation

Submitted records are automatically sorted and organised into a structured case folder before they reach the specialist. This means the reviewing physician can focus entirely on clinical assessment rather than document management. AI assists with:

  • ✓Clinical timelines
  • ✓Biomarker extraction
  • ✓Record organization

Dedicated Case Support

Each patient receives personalized coordination with an assigned case manager and clinical oncology navigator. Support includes:

  • ✓Record coordination
  • ✓Consultation preparation
  • ✓Follow-up communication

Medical interpretation and treatment recommendations are written by the reviewing physician.

Medical interpretation and treatment recommendations are written by the reviewing physician. Technology enhances efficiency while clinical expertise remains central.

Cancer treatment evolves rapidly. Your review reflects it.

Every consultation considers current evidence-based treatments, targeted therapies, and clinical trial opportunities. When appropriate, patients may also receive information about medication assistance programs.

Our Mission

"Cancer decisions are among the most important decisions patients will ever make."

Cancer Clarity MD was created to provide patients and families with access to experienced oncology specialists who can review complex cases and deliver clear, evidence-based guidance. Patients remain under the care of their treating physicians while benefiting from additional oncology insight and global medical expertise.

Frequently Asked Questions

Common questions

Everything patients and families ask before getting started.

About the Service
A second opinion is an independent review of a cancer diagnosis or treatment plan by a specialist who was not involved in your initial care. It can confirm a diagnosis, identify additional treatment options, evaluate targeted therapies or clinical trials, or simply provide reassurance about current care. Studies show that oncology second opinions result in meaningful changes to diagnosis or treatment recommendations in a significant proportion of cases.
No. Cancer Clarity MD provides independent specialist guidance only. Patients remain under the care of their treating physicians. Our service is designed to complement - not replace - your existing oncology team. The written opinion you receive is intended to be shared with your treating oncologist to support shared decision-making.
Your case is reviewed by Dr. Nikki Bajaj, MD, a board-certified hematologist-oncologist with expertise in both benign and malignant hematology, solid tumors, and emerging therapies including targeted agents and clinical trial evaluation. Every case receives dedicated specialist attention - there is no delegation to non-physician reviewers.
Yes. Patients who select the Written Opinion + Video Consultation package ($895) will have a direct video consultation with Dr. Bajaj to review the written findings, ask questions, and discuss next steps. This consultation is scheduled at a time convenient for you, regardless of your time zone.
Records & Process
Helpful records typically include: pathology reports (biopsy and surgical), molecular or genomic testing results, imaging reports (CT, MRI, PET), laboratory results, physician clinic notes, and treatment history. You do not need to submit every record - our team will review what you provide and follow up if additional records are needed before the review begins. Turnaround time begins once records are confirmed complete.
Most written reports are delivered within 48 to 72 hours after all records are received and confirmed complete. Expedited review (within 24 hours) is available as an add-on upon request. The video consultation, if included, is scheduled after the written report is complete at a time that works for you.
After completing the intake form, you will receive instructions for secure document upload. You may also coordinate record transfer directly with your treating physicians. Our team can assist with record coordination if needed - this is included as part of the service. Records may be submitted as PDFs, scanned documents, or direct electronic transfer from your medical center.
Yes. Medical records and personal information are submitted through encrypted, privacy-protected systems. Cancer Clarity MD maintains strict data security and confidentiality practices. Your information is never shared with third parties for commercial purposes.
Insurance & Payment
Cancer Clarity MD is currently a self-pay service. We do not bill insurance directly, and this service is not covered by Medicare or Medicaid. However, some patients have successfully sought partial reimbursement from private insurers by submitting receipts directly. We recommend checking with your insurance provider. A detailed invoice/receipt can be provided upon request to support any reimbursement submission.
Payment is processed securely online. Major credit and debit cards are accepted. International payments are also accepted. Payment is collected after intake form submission and prior to the start of the review. If you have questions about payment, please contact us at [email protected].
If the specialist determines that your case falls outside the scope of our service before the review begins, you will receive a full refund. Once the specialist review has commenced, fees are non-refundable. If you have concerns about your case or the service, please contact us directly at [email protected].
International Patients
Yes. Cancer Clarity MD is designed for patients worldwide. No travel is required. Medical records are submitted digitally, the written report is delivered electronically, and video consultations are conducted through a secure video platform accessible from anywhere. Reports and consultations are available in English, Spanish, Hindi, and Gujarati.
Yes. Records from international medical centers are accepted. Records in languages other than English should ideally be accompanied by certified translations where possible. Our team will work with you if translation or record formatting is needed. Please contact us if you have questions about submitting records from a specific country or healthcare system.
Sample Report

See What a Second Opinion Looks Like

The following is an example of the written second opinion report patients receive. All names and case details are illustrative only.

Download a PDF version of this sample report to share with your care team. Download Sample Report
SAMPLE
Cancer Clarity MD
Independent Oncology Second Opinion Consultation
Case ID: CC-2026-001
Date of Review: October 15, 2025
Age / Sex: 63 / Male
Reviewing Specialist: Dr. Nikki Bajaj, MD
Primary Diagnosis: Metastatic Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer (NSCLC)
Specialist Credential: Board-Certified Hematology and Medical Oncology
Records Reviewed
  • Pathology report confirming lung adenocarcinoma
  • Molecular / next-generation sequencing (NGS) panel showing KRAS G12C mutation
  • PET-CT imaging reports (most recent: September 2025)
  • Medical oncology clinic notes from treating institution
  • Prior treatment records including platinum-based chemotherapy and pembrolizumab
Clinical Summary

The patient is a 63-year-old male diagnosed with stage IV lung adenocarcinoma with metastatic involvement identified on PET imaging. Molecular profiling via NGS demonstrated a KRAS G12C mutation (VAF 42%). PD-L1 expression was 35%. Initial treatment included platinum-based chemotherapy combined with pembrolizumab. Restaging imaging after four cycles demonstrates disease progression with new hepatic lesions.

Key Findings
  • Pathology confirms metastatic lung adenocarcinoma - diagnosis is consistent with the submitted records.
  • Molecular testing identifies KRAS G12C mutation, which may allow consideration of targeted therapies approved or in clinical development for this alteration.
  • Clinical history and restaging imaging are consistent with progression following first-line chemo-immunotherapy.
  • PD-L1 expression (35%) was insufficient for single-agent pembrolizumab as first-line therapy per current guidelines - combination therapy was appropriate.
Evidence-Based Treatment Considerations

Potential options in the second-line setting may include:

  • KRAS G12C targeted therapy such as sotorasib (Lumakras) or adagrasib (Krazati), which have demonstrated activity in this molecular subset
  • Participation in clinical trials evaluating emerging KRAS-directed agents or combination strategies
  • Alternative systemic therapy depending on prior response, toxicity profile, and current performance status
Final treatment decisions should be made in consultation with the treating oncologist who has full knowledge of the patient's current clinical condition, comorbidities, and preferences.
Questions to Discuss with Treating Oncologist
  • Is targeted therapy with a KRAS G12C inhibitor appropriate at this stage, and what is the preferred agent?
  • Are there clinical trials available at this institution or nearby centers that may be suitable?
  • Would additional molecular testing (e.g., STK11, KEAP1 co-mutations) provide useful treatment stratification information?
  • Should repeat biopsy be considered to assess for resistance mechanisms?
Summary

Based on the records reviewed, the diagnosis of metastatic lung adenocarcinoma with KRAS G12C mutation is confirmed. Following progression on first-line chemo-immunotherapy, targeted therapy directed at the KRAS G12C mutation may represent a reasonable second-line option. Clinical trial evaluation may also be appropriate. Discussion with the treating oncologist is recommended to determine the most appropriate next step.

Dr. Nikki Bajaj
Dr. Nikki Bajaj, MD
Board-Certified Hematology and Medical Oncology
Cancer Clarity MD
Important Note

This report is based solely on the records provided for review. It is intended for educational and advisory purposes and does not replace direct care from the patient's treating physicians. Cancer Clarity MD provides independent medical review and does not establish a primary physician-patient treatment relationship. Case details are illustrative (SAMPLE only).

Medical Disclaimer

Cancer Clarity MD provides independent medical review and educational guidance based on the records submitted. The information provided through this service is intended for educational and informational purposes only and should not be considered a diagnosis, treatment prescription, or medical directive.

This service does not establish a physician-patient treatment relationship and does not replace care from the patient's treating physicians. Patients should always consult their primary oncology team before making medical decisions.

Cancer Clarity MD does not provide emergency medical services.

If you are experiencing a medical emergency, please call 911 or seek immediate medical care.
